summary: Matched and linked the item draw translation unit by reshaping src/melee/it/itdraw.c, especially it_8026EECC and its static inline helpers, then marking melee/it/itdraw.c as Matching in configure.py. The decomp.dev report credited 2 new matches in main/melee/it/itdraw: it_8026EECC reached 100.00% and .sdata reached 100.00%, with GALE01 totals improving by +1248 matched-code bytes and +2196 linked-code bytes.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/itdraw.c") became Object(Matching, "melee/it/itdraw.c"), linking the unit as matching.
Refactored static inline helpers around it_8026EECC in src/melee/it/itdraw.c.
Replaced several direct gobj->user_data Item* loads with GET_ITEM(gobj).
Collapsed temporary Vec3* new_pos variables and if/else assignments into direct ternary arguments: ip->xDCF_flag.b7 ? pos : NULL.
Extracted duplicated Camera_80031060() switch logic into static inline it_8026EECC_inline_sw(HSD_GObj* gobj, s32 arg1, Vec3* pos).
Removed the explicit PAD_STACK(48) from it_8026EECC after the refactor produced the needed stack/code shape without it.
Updated GALE01 symbols: it_804D5168 size changed from 0x8 to 0x4, and it_804DC6B0 was renamed/reclassified from global it_804DC6B0 to local @275.
smart_moves: Factoring the repeated Camera_80031060 switch into a static inline helper let the source express one logical branch while still giving the matcher a reusable inline shape.
Using GET_ITEM(gobj) instead of raw gobj->user_data aligned this file with project item-access conventions and affected the emitted code enough to match.
Passing ip->xDCF_flag.b7 ? pos : NULL directly avoided local pointer temporaries that were likely perturbing register/stack allocation.
The symbol-map edits were limited and targeted: a corrected .sdata object size and a local .sdata2 float label, rather than broad symbol churn.
The PR did not just match the function locally; it also flipped the build configuration to Matching and verified linked-code progress.
decomp_lessons: For item code, prefer GET_ITEM(gobj) when the surrounding code expects the standard item accessor; direct gobj->user_data can produce a different matching shape.
Inlining strategy matters: moving duplicated switch logic into a static inline wrapper can improve matching while preserving source readability.
Ternary pointer selection can be a useful codegen tactic when an if-initialized temporary pointer creates the wrong stack or register behavior.
When a function match depends on nearby small data, audit symbols.txt for object size and local/global classification; a code match may require matching .sdata metadata too.
If a refactor naturally removes the need for PAD_STACK, prefer the cleaner source rather than retaining artificial padding.
naming_conventions: Unidentified local small-data symbols use @NNN labels, shown by it_804DC6B0 becoming @275.
Global unidentified item symbols retain it_804Dxxxx names only when they are truly global, shown by it_804D5168 remaining global but with corrected size.
Helper names followed the existing function-address-derived convention, e.g. it_8026EECC_inline_0 through it_8026EECC_inline_3 and new it_8026EECC_inline_sw.
Project item access convention is GET_ITEM(gobj) rather than open-coded gobj->user_data where practical.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace local pointer temporary plus conditional assignment with a direct conditional-expression function argument.
Extract duplicated switch-over-camera-state logic into a static inline helper to influence inlining and duplication patterns.
Use GET_ITEM(gobj) consistently to guide load/cast codegen for Item* access.
Remove PAD_STACK once stack layout is achieved through source structure.
Synchronize configure.py Matching/NonMatching status only after the translation unit reaches matching/linkable state.
Correct small-data symbol metadata when .sdata matching reports indicate remaining data mismatch.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
Automated decomp.dev report confirmed GALE01 improvements: matched code 71.54% (+0.03%, +1248 bytes), linked code 46.01% (+0.06%, +2196 bytes), matched data +8 bytes, and linked data +16 bytes.
Automated report listed 2 new matches: main/melee/it/itdraw it_8026EECC from 99.41% to 100.00%, and main/melee/it/itdraw .sdata from 66.67% to 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find other item files using direct gobj->user_data where GET_ITEM(gobj) may improve matching.
Search for duplicated Camera_80031060 switch blocks that might benefit from static inline extraction.
Audit remaining NonMatching melee/it units for small-data symbol size or local/global classification mismatches.
Look for PAD_STACK usages in item draw/collision code that may become unnecessary after source-structure refactors.
